ops.text: PRELIMINARY ENCODER SIGNAL (pending DeLong + 3-seed variance + independent VERIFY — do NOT treat as final): on the frozen 1325 gate frame, Ettin-150m = 0.7643 and ModernBERT-base = 0.7526, BOTH above incumbent 0.7161 / TF-IDF 0.7182 / rep-stats 0.7083. If these survive paired DeLong + the variance check, transformer encoders DO beat the TF-IDF-level gate by ~+0.04-0.05 — a real "different architecture helps" result (the user's directive), and the campaign's first clear architectural win. Ettin (the smaller 150m model) is the best arm. TWO BUGS being fixed before close: (1) DeBERTaV3 NaN-diverged (loss→nan, grad_norm nan, eval_auc 0 — classic DeBERTa-v3 MPS/LR instability; fix = lr 5e-6 + fp32, else CPU, else mark failed-diverged and proceed with ettin+modernbert). (2) E43's encoder scored a degenerate 0/0 on eval_a (below majority 0.408 — a label-index/pred-file bug, not a real result) and eval_b was empty — being diagnosed + re-scored. Fix agent running; then the resumable driver re-launches detached (skips the banked ettin/modernbert), completes deberta + E43 + variance + DeLong, and VERIFY gates both.
